label: Attract
core: How you pull engineers toward you

seed.reality: Your brand IS the founders. Nobody's heard of you. Every coffee chat is a recruiting pitch.

goals.text: Founders' network generates 80%+ of candidate pipeline
A compelling 'why join us' narrative that converts in one conversation
Active presence in 2-3 relevant engineering communities

metrics.name: Network-sourced pipeline
metrics.why: Reveals if your network is your only channel

metrics.name: Referral response rate
metrics.why: Measures founder credibility in the talent market

metrics.name: Offer-to-close time
metrics.why: Speed is your biggest advantage over larger companies

artifacts.title: Founding Story One-Pager
artifacts.desc: Why this, why now, why us — in 200 words

seed.dx: Your GitHub repo IS your tech brand. README quality matters more than a careers page.
avoid: Building a careers page before PMF

startup.reality: Competing with known companies for talent. Your edge: speed, mission, equity, and the chance to build from scratch.

goals.text: First engineering blog post — real, technical, authentic
Referral program live, generating 30%+ of pipeline
Glassdoor / LinkedIn shows genuine engineering culture
First open-source project from the team

metrics.name: Inbound vs outbound ratio
metrics.why: Shows if your brand pulls talent or you chase it

metrics.name: Referral pipeline share
metrics.why: High referrals signal genuine team satisfaction

metrics.name: Eng blog posts / month
metrics.why: Content velocity builds compounding brand awareness

metrics.name: Candidate NPS
metrics.why: Bad candidate experience poisons your talent market fast

artifacts.title: Blog Kickstart Guide
artifacts.desc: First 5 posts that actually attract engineers

artifacts.title: Tech Stack Decision Log
artifacts.desc: Public 'why we chose X' for credibility

startup.dx: Your DevEx is a recruiting tool. Engineers talk. A great local dev experience travels by word of mouth.
avoid: Competing on salary alone vs equity + mission

scaleup.reality: Founders' network is tapped out. You need a systematic engine. Brand must scale beyond personal connections.

goals.text: Engineering blog drives 20%+ of qualified inbound
2+ conference talks per quarter from team engineers
Tech Radar published and maintained quarterly
Open-source project with external contributors

metrics.name: Inbound quality score
metrics.why: Volume without quality wastes recruiter bandwidth

metrics.name: Blog → Apply conversion
metrics.why: Connects content investment to actual pipeline ROI

metrics.name: Conference submissions / qtr
metrics.why: Tracks team willingness to represent the brand publicly

metrics.name: Cost per quality hire
metrics.why: Ensures brand investment reduces acquisition cost over time

metrics.name: Glassdoor eng rating
metrics.why: Candidates check Glassdoor before responding to outreach

artifacts.title: OSS Contribution Policy
artifacts.desc: When and how to contribute upstream

scaleup.dx: Backstage or IDP becomes a recruiting differentiator. 'We invest in DevEx' is a real competitive advantage.

growth.reality: Hiring at volume. Brand needs to be a machine. Multiple sub-brands by team and domain.

goals.text: Top-10 in relevant 'Best Engineering Teams' rankings
Engineering brand awareness > 40% in target talent pools
Dedicated DevRel / Eng Marketing team of 3+
University partnership pipeline with 5+ schools

metrics.name: Brand awareness (target pool)
metrics.why: Unmeasured brand is just hope disguised as strategy

metrics.name: GitHub org stars
metrics.why: Proxy for external developer mindshare and credibility

metrics.name: Inbound quality pipeline
metrics.why: Flipping to inbound-dominant cuts cost and boosts quality

metrics.name: University pipeline yield
metrics.why: Early-career pipeline secures talent before competitors do

metrics.name: Blog monthly readers
metrics.why: Audience size drives top-of-funnel for passive candidates

growth.dx: Your IDP, Tech Radar, and eng practices become externally published assets. Think Spotify model, Netflix culture deck.

enterprise.reality: Defending brand while innovating. Managing 'too corporate' perception. Multiple geos and business units.

goals.text: Perceived as 'innovative' despite scale (annual brand survey)
Major OSS project adopted externally (1K+ stars)
Engineering podcast / video series: 50K+ monthly audience
Cross-acquisition talent pipeline > 10% of mobility

metrics.name: Innovation perception
metrics.why: Enterprises die when top talent sees them as boring

metrics.name: OSS external adoption
metrics.why: Proves engineering impact extends beyond the company

metrics.name: Content monthly reach
metrics.why: Sustained reach keeps you relevant in a noisy market

metrics.name: Attrition to FAANG/startups
metrics.why: Signals whether your brand competes at the top tier

metrics.name: Internal brand satisfaction
metrics.why: External brand rings hollow if engineers inside disagree

artifacts.title: OSS Strategy & Governance
artifacts.desc: What to open-source, how to sustain

enterprise.dx: At forest scale, your DevEx platform IS a product. Backstage instances, internal SDKs, and platform blogs become external assets.

label: Select
core: How you evaluate and close engineers

seed.reality: You're co-founder dating. Culture fit and raw ability matter more than a process.

goals.text: Every hire does a paid trial day or pairing session
Close candidates within 5 days of first conversation

metrics.name: Time to decision
metrics.why: Slow decisions lose candidates to faster-moving startups

metrics.name: Trial-to-offer rate
metrics.why: Low conversion means your trial filters signal or scares talent

artifacts.title: Paid Trial Day Structure
artifacts.desc: What to work on, how to evaluate

seed.dx: Give candidates repo access during trial. Their reaction to your codebase IS the signal.

startup.reality: First interview structure. First rubrics. Balancing speed with signal quality.

goals.text: Structured rubrics for technical + values assessment
Time-to-hire ≤ 15 days
Work-sample > whiteboard for all technical rounds
Offer acceptance ≥ 85%

metrics.name: Time to hire
metrics.why: Every extra day increases drop-off to competing offers

metrics.name: Offer acceptance rate
metrics.why: Low acceptance exposes comp or culture misalignment

metrics.name: Candidate NPS
metrics.why: Rejected candidates still talk about your process publicly

metrics.name: Quality of hire (6mo)
metrics.why: Validates whether interview signals predict real performance

startup.dx: Dev env setup speed is a hiring signal. If candidates struggle, new hires will too.

scaleup.reality: Scaling quality at volume. Interviewer training. Cross-manager calibration becomes critical.

goals.text: All interviewers trained + certified, IRR > 0.65
Time-to-hire ≤ 25 days at 5+ hires/month
Score-first debriefs: individual scores submitted before group discussion
Diversity tracked at every funnel stage

metrics.name: Time to hire
metrics.why: Proves you can scale hiring without sacrificing speed

metrics.name: Inter-rater reliability
metrics.why: Low IRR means your bar depends on who interviews

metrics.name: Offer acceptance
metrics.why: Declining acceptance at volume signals systemic closing gaps

metrics.name: Diversity pipeline %
metrics.why: Pipeline diversity is the leading indicator for team diversity

metrics.name: Candidate NPS
metrics.why: Process quality at scale separates you from competitors

scaleup.dx: Start measuring time-to-productive as a hire quality signal. Connect recruiting → onboard → ship data.

growth.reality: 10-20 hires/month. Hiring committees. Dedicated recruiting ops. Process is a product.

goals.text: Hiring committee model for IC4+ and all manager roles
Time-to-hire stable ≤ 28 days despite volume
Automated pipeline analytics with conversion tracking
Published compensation philosophy

metrics.name: Time to hire (at volume)
metrics.why: Stable speed despite volume proves process maturity

metrics.name: Committee agreement rate
metrics.why: Disagreement reveals calibration drift across teams

metrics.name: Screen → offer conversion
metrics.why: Funnel efficiency prevents recruiter burnout at scale

metrics.name: 1yr quality of hire
metrics.why: Links hiring decisions to long-term business outcomes

metrics.name: Offer competitiveness
metrics.why: Below-market offers waste pipeline you paid to build

growth.dx: Hiring data connects to perf data. Which interview signals predict 1-year success?

enterprise.reality: Global machine. Regulatory complexity. University programs. Internal mobility competes with external hiring.

goals.text: Global standards with local adaptation framework
Internal mobility fills 25%+ of open roles
AI-assisted screening with published bias audits
University pipeline yields 20%+ of entry-level

metrics.name: Global time-to-fill
metrics.why: Consistency across geos prevents regional talent bottlenecks

metrics.name: Internal fill rate
metrics.why: Internal moves cost less and retain institutional knowledge

metrics.name: AI bias audit score
metrics.why: Unaudited AI screening creates legal and ethical liability

metrics.name: Process compliance
metrics.why: Non-compliance across jurisdictions invites regulatory risk

metrics.name: University yield
metrics.why: Measures ROI on early-career talent investment programs

enterprise.dx: Enterprise ATS integrates with IDP. New hires auto-provisioned into Backstage on Day 1.
avoid: One global process ignoring local context

label: Launch
core: How fast new engineers reach first impact

seed.reality: No onboarding. There's 'here's the repo, here's Slack, let's pair on something real.'

goals.text: New engineer ships code on Day 1
Full architecture context within Week 1 via pairing

metrics.name: Time to first commit
metrics.why: Early contribution builds confidence and belonging fast

metrics.name: Context ramp (self-report)
metrics.why: Unspoken tribal knowledge is the real onboarding blocker

seed.dx: If your dev setup takes > 30 minutes, fix that before hiring anyone else.

startup.reality: First real onboarding structure. Buddy system starts. Written architecture docs appear.

goals.text: Dev environment fully operational within 2 hours
First PR merged within 3 business days
30-day ramp checklist with buddy accountability

metrics.name: Dev env setup time
metrics.why: Setup friction predicts new-hire frustration and early doubt

metrics.name: Time to first PR
metrics.why: First PR is a proxy for how quickly someone feels productive

metrics.name: Day 30 NPS
metrics.why: Early dissatisfaction predicts 90-day attrition

artifacts.title: Architecture Overview
artifacts.desc: System context, data flow, key decisions

startup.dx: Automate dev env setup. Every hour saved here pays back on every future hire.

scaleup.reality: Structured 30-60-90. Platform engineering starts owning the dev environment. Consistency matters.

goals.text: Dev env self-service ≤ 4 hours (platform-owned)
First PR merged within 5 days with structured review
Architecture deep-dive program by Day 14
Day 90 ramp score ≥ 85%

metrics.name: Dev env spin-up time
metrics.why: Platform-owned setup proves your golden paths actually work

metrics.name: Time to first PR
metrics.why: Slower at scale reveals process bloat in onboarding

metrics.name: Day 90 ramp score
metrics.why: Measures if structured onboarding delivers real competence

metrics.name: 90-day attrition
metrics.why: Early exits are the most expensive hiring failures

metrics.name: Day 30 NPS
metrics.why: Captures onboarding quality while experience is still fresh

scaleup.dx: Backstage/IDP onboarding: new engineer → scaffolded service → first deploy in hours.

growth.reality: Onboarding is a product with its own team. Cohort-based. Engineering Academy.

goals.text: Cohort-based onboarding for 10+ engineers/month
Zero-touch dev env < 2 hours (platform SLO)
2-week Engineering Academy technical ramp
Buddy and manager NPS both > 4.5

metrics.name: Dev env SLO
metrics.why: SLO framing forces platform to treat onboarding as production

metrics.name: Day 90 ramp score
metrics.why: Validates the academy model delivers consistent ramp quality

metrics.name: Academy satisfaction
metrics.why: Unhappy cohorts signal curriculum drift from real work

metrics.name: Buddy NPS
metrics.why: Buddy quality is the hidden variable in onboarding success

metrics.name: 90-day attrition
metrics.why: At volume, even small early attrition rates compound cost

growth.dx: Platform team owns onboarding DevEx as an SLO. Dev env time tracked like uptime.

enterprise.reality: Global infrastructure. Multi-geo, multi-BU. Onboarding analytics predict retention.

goals.text: Global standard with BU/geo customization framework
Onboarding signals predict 1-year retention (80%+ accuracy)
Zero-touch global dev env provisioning < 1 hour
First production deploy within 10 business days

metrics.name: Global dev env SLO
metrics.why: Global provisioning gaps create unequal engineer experiences

metrics.name: Retention prediction accuracy
metrics.why: Predicting attrition early enables targeted intervention

metrics.name: Time to first prod deploy
metrics.why: Production impact speed measures real onboarding effectiveness

metrics.name: Global ramp score
metrics.why: Consistency across BUs ensures brand promise matches reality

metrics.name: Standard compliance
metrics.why: Non-compliance fragments the onboarding experience at scale

enterprise.dx: Enterprise IDP provisions everything: repos, CI/CD, monitoring, access — one catalog entry.

label: Equip
core: The tools, platforms, and environments that multiply engineering output

seed.reality: Everyone uses whatever works. One shared staging server. Deploys are manual. It's fine — for now.

goals.text: Single-command local dev setup that works on every machine
CI pipeline runs tests on every PR
Shared decisions on core stack documented in ADRs

metrics.name: Dev env setup time
metrics.why: Every minute of setup friction slows your tiny team down

metrics.name: CI pass rate
metrics.why: Flaky CI erodes trust in automation before habits form

metrics.name: Deploy frequency
metrics.why: Daily deploys prove your toolchain enables rapid iteration

artifacts.title: Dev Setup Script
artifacts.desc: One command to rule them all

seed.dx: Your toolchain IS your DevEx. At this stage, the fastest path between idea and production is everything.

startup.reality: First infrastructure decisions that will last years. Staging environments, observability basics, secrets management.

goals.text: Reproducible environments (dev, staging, prod)
Basic observability: logs, errors, uptime
Secrets management — not in env files or repos
Feature flag system for safe rollouts

metrics.name: Env parity issues / month
metrics.why: Parity bugs waste hours debugging non-production problems

metrics.name: Observability coverage
metrics.why: Blind spots in monitoring become outages in production

metrics.name: Secrets in code (scan)
metrics.why: One leaked secret can compromise your entire infrastructure

metrics.name: Deploy frequency
metrics.why: Multiple daily deploys prove your infra supports team velocity

startup.dx: Every 'works on my machine' bug is a DevEx failure. Reproducible environments pay for themselves immediately.

scaleup.reality: Multiple teams need independent velocity. Platform thinking emerges. Golden paths vs. wild west.

goals.text: Golden path: new service from template to production in < 1 day
Centralized CI/CD with team-level customization
Service catalog with ownership, runbooks, SLOs
Cost visibility by team / service

metrics.name: New service lead time
metrics.why: Slow service creation forces teams into shared monoliths

metrics.name: Golden path adoption
metrics.why: Low adoption means your golden paths miss real team needs

metrics.name: CI/CD pipeline time
metrics.why: Slow pipelines kill deploy frequency and iteration speed

metrics.name: Service catalog coverage
metrics.why: Uncatalogued services become orphans nobody can maintain

metrics.name: Infra cost per engineer
metrics.why: Makes cloud spend visible before it spirals unnoticed

scaleup.dx: The platform team's customers are the other engineers. Treat golden paths like a product with adoption metrics.

growth.reality: Internal Developer Platform is a first-class product. Multiple teams build on shared foundations. Backstage or equivalent.

goals.text: IDP with self-service provisioning, catalog, and docs
Platform NPS > 40 from engineering teams
Automated compliance and security scanning in CI
Cost optimization program saving 15%+ annually

metrics.name: Platform NPS
metrics.why: Engineers route around unloved platforms, fragmenting standards

metrics.name: Self-service adoption
metrics.why: Low self-service means teams still depend on platform tickets

metrics.name: Security scan coverage
metrics.why: Gaps in scanning are gaps in your security posture

metrics.name: Cost per transaction
metrics.why: Unit economics reveal if platform scales efficiently

metrics.name: Platform incident rate
metrics.why: Platform outages multiply across every team that depends on it

growth.dx: A mature IDP means engineers think about their domain, not infrastructure. Cognitive load is the metric.

enterprise.reality: Multi-cloud, multi-region, multi-BU. Platform federation. Standards across hundreds of teams.

goals.text: Federated platform: global standards, local extensions
< 5 min from code merge to production (p95)
Cross-BU platform reuse > 60%
Platform engineering as a competitive moat

metrics.name: Merge to production (p95)
metrics.why: Deployment speed at enterprise proves platform doesn't add drag

metrics.name: Platform reuse across BUs
metrics.why: Low reuse means BUs rebuild what already exists

metrics.name: Global platform NPS
metrics.why: Enterprise platforms fail when mandated but unloved

metrics.name: Compliance automation
metrics.why: Manual compliance gates become the top shipping bottleneck

metrics.name: Infra cost efficiency
metrics.why: Year-over-year gains justify continued platform investment

artifacts.title: Multi-Cloud Strategy
artifacts.desc: When and why to go multi-cloud

enterprise.dx: At enterprise scale, your IDP competes with public cloud DevEx. Engineers choose your platform because it's genuinely better for their use case.

label: Grow
core: How engineers develop skills, advance careers, and find meaning

seed.reality: Growth = the company growing. Everyone's learning by building. No ladders, no levels, just shipping.

goals.text: Regular 1:1s with founders about growth and direction
Learning budget — even $500/person signals investment
Explicit 'what you'll learn here' pitch for each role

metrics.name: 1:1 frequency
metrics.why: Inconsistent 1:1s signal founders deprioritize people

metrics.name: Learning budget utilization
metrics.why: Unused budget means growth is talked about, not acted on

metrics.name: Growth satisfaction
metrics.why: Early hires leave when they stop learning

seed.dx: Side projects, open-source contributions, and conference talks ARE the growth program at this stage.

startup.reality: First career conversations. Engineers wonder 'what's my path here?' First tech lead promotions.

goals.text: Lightweight engineering ladder: IC track (3-4 levels)
First tech lead role defined and filled internally
Semi-annual growth conversations with written takeaways
Conference / course budget actively used

metrics.name: Ladder clarity score
metrics.why: Ambiguous paths drive engineers to companies with clear ones

metrics.name: Internal promotions
metrics.why: Visible promotions prove growth is real, not theoretical

metrics.name: Growth conversation cadence
metrics.why: Without rhythm, career growth becomes an afterthought

metrics.name: Learning budget usage
metrics.why: Low usage suggests the process is too bureaucratic to access

startup.dx: Internal tech talks, code review culture, and pair programming are growth infrastructure disguised as practices.

scaleup.reality: Dual-track career paths matter now. IC5/Staff roles needed. First performance calibrations. Mentoring programs.

goals.text: Full IC + Management dual-track ladder
Calibration process for fair, consistent leveling
Mentorship program matching senior ↔ junior
Internal mobility: engineers can move teams

metrics.name: Dual-track clarity
metrics.why: Engineers leave when the IC path feels second-class

metrics.name: Calibration completion
metrics.why: Skipped calibrations create inconsistent promotion bars

metrics.name: Mentorship pairs active
metrics.why: Mentorship accelerates junior growth and senior retention

metrics.name: Internal mobility rate
metrics.why: Team-hoarding talent leads to attrition, not loyalty

metrics.name: Promo cycle satisfaction
metrics.why: Frustrating promos erode trust in the entire growth system

scaleup.dx: Engineers who teach — through docs, tooling, or mentoring — should be recognized on the IC track.

growth.reality: Career development at scale. Engineering academy. Staff+ pipeline. Succession planning begins.

goals.text: Staff+ pipeline: identified, sponsored, tracked
Engineering-wide learning platform (internal + external)
Succession plans for all Director+ roles
360 feedback integrated into growth cycles

metrics.name: Staff+ pipeline depth
metrics.why: Thin pipeline forces external hires for senior IC roles

metrics.name: Learning platform engagement
metrics.why: Unused learning platforms are expensive shelfware

metrics.name: Succession plan coverage
metrics.why: Ungapped succession prevents leadership vacuum surprises

metrics.name: 360 feedback completion
metrics.why: Incomplete feedback leaves growth plans based on guesswork

metrics.name: Growth satisfaction
metrics.why: Satisfied engineers stay; dissatisfied ones interview quietly

growth.dx: Internal platforms that engineers build become Staff+ project vehicles. Growing the IDP grows careers.

enterprise.reality: Talent as strategic asset. Engineering fellows. Cross-BU rotation programs. Leadership at every level.

goals.text: Distinguished / Fellow track with external impact
Cross-BU rotation program with 15%+ participation
Engineering leadership development cohorts (annual)
Skills taxonomy mapped to business capabilities

metrics.name: Fellow/Distinguished pipeline
metrics.why: Top ICs leave when there is no apex career destination

metrics.name: Rotation program participation
metrics.why: Cross-BU movement builds organizational resilience

metrics.name: Leadership cohort satisfaction
metrics.why: Poor leadership programs produce poor engineering leaders

metrics.name: Skills taxonomy coverage
metrics.why: Unmapped skills make workforce planning guesswork

metrics.name: Internal promotion rate
metrics.why: External-heavy fills signal broken internal growth paths

enterprise.dx: Distinguished engineers shape the IDP roadmap. Platform strategy and career growth become inseparable.

label: Ship
core: How code moves from idea to production safely and fast

seed.reality: You deploy from a laptop. Main branch is production. Rollbacks are git reverts. It works because you're small.

goals.text: Deployments happen at least daily
Basic CI: tests pass before merge
One-click (or one-command) deploy

metrics.name: Deploy frequency
metrics.why: Daily shipping proves your process enables rapid learning

metrics.name: Lead time for changes
metrics.why: Long lead times mean ideas die waiting in the queue

metrics.name: Change failure rate
metrics.why: High failure rate erodes confidence in shipping fast

artifacts.title: Rollback Procedure
artifacts.desc: How to undo a bad deploy

seed.dx: If deploying feels scary, something is wrong. Ship small, ship often. Fear of deployment = DevEx debt.

startup.reality: First CI/CD pipeline. PR reviews. Staging environment. Feature flags appear. Release cadence matters.

goals.text: Automated CI/CD pipeline for all services
PR review turnaround ≤ 4 hours
Feature flags for risky changes
Basic incident response process

metrics.name: Deploy frequency
metrics.why: Increasing frequency validates your CI/CD investment

metrics.name: Lead time for changes
metrics.why: Measures whether new process helps or hinders delivery

metrics.name: PR review turnaround
metrics.why: Review bottlenecks are the top velocity killer at this stage

metrics.name: Change failure rate
metrics.why: Tracks if automation catches problems before production

startup.dx: PR review speed is the single biggest shipping bottleneck at this stage. Measure and optimize it.

scaleup.reality: Multiple teams shipping independently. DORA metrics tracked. Deployment pipelines are team-owned. Incident management formalizes.

goals.text: DORA metrics tracked and reviewed monthly
Team-owned deployment pipelines (not centralized gate)
Structured incident management with blameless post-mortems
Progressive delivery: canary, blue-green, or ring deploys

metrics.name: Deploy frequency
metrics.why: On-demand deploys prove teams own their own release cadence

metrics.name: Lead time
metrics.why: Short lead time at scale proves decoupled team delivery

metrics.name: Change failure rate
metrics.why: Low rate validates progressive delivery strategies work

metrics.name: MTTR
metrics.why: Recovery speed matters more than preventing every failure

metrics.name: Post-mortem completion
metrics.why: Skipped post-mortems repeat the same incidents

scaleup.dx: Developer experience IS delivery performance. If the inner loop is slow, DORA metrics will show it.

growth.reality: Shipping at scale. Release engineering team. Feature management platform. SLO-driven reliability.

goals.text: SLO-driven reliability: every service has error budgets
Elite DORA performance (per team)
Automated rollback on SLO breach
Release engineering as a discipline

metrics.name: DORA: Elite teams
metrics.why: Elite DORA is the benchmark for world-class delivery orgs

metrics.name: SLO coverage
metrics.why: Services without SLOs have no shared definition of healthy

metrics.name: Error budget utilization
metrics.why: Connects reliability investment directly to shipping speed

metrics.name: Automated rollback rate
metrics.why: Manual rollbacks at scale add minutes that cost revenue

metrics.name: Release incident rate
metrics.why: Release-caused incidents undermine confidence in shipping

growth.dx: Error budgets connect reliability to velocity. When the budget's healthy, ship fast. When it's burning, slow down and fix.

enterprise.reality: Global delivery. Regulatory gates. Multi-region deployments. Supply chain security. Compliance as code.

goals.text: Compliance-as-code: regulatory gates automated in CI
Global deployment: multi-region with < 5 min rollout
Software supply chain security (SBOM, signing)
Chaos engineering program

metrics.name: Global deploy time
metrics.why: Slow global deploys create pressure to batch risky changes

metrics.name: Compliance automation
metrics.why: Manual compliance is the enterprise tax on engineering velocity

metrics.name: SBOM coverage
metrics.why: Supply chain attacks exploit exactly the gaps you haven't mapped

metrics.name: Chaos experiment frequency
metrics.why: Regular chaos builds resilience before real failures test it

metrics.name: Global MTTR
metrics.why: Enterprise recovery speed directly correlates with revenue impact

enterprise.dx: At enterprise scale, compliance gates must be invisible to developers — baked into the platform, not bolted on.

label: Thrive
core: How you keep engineers engaged, healthy, and choosing to stay

seed.reality: Retention = excitement. Everyone's bought in. But burnout risk is real when you're sprinting 24/7.

goals.text: Sustainable pace: no glorification of overwork
Weekly or biweekly check-ins on energy and workload
Equity and comp conversations are transparent

metrics.name: Team energy (self-report)
metrics.why: Low energy is the earliest signal of unsustainable pace

metrics.name: Working hours/week
metrics.why: Chronic overwork predicts burnout and eventual departures

metrics.name: Zero attrition
metrics.why: Any voluntary exit at seed stage is an existential risk

seed.dx: Deploy frequency drops when people burn out. DevEx metrics are retention leading indicators.

startup.reality: First attrition stings. Engagement survey appears. Benefits start mattering. Culture is tested under pressure.

goals.text: Quarterly pulse survey with action commitments
Competitive benefits package (health, learning, flexibility)
Retention conversations with flight-risk engineers
Team events and rituals that aren't forced

metrics.name: Engagement score
metrics.why: Engagement drops before attrition spikes, giving you lead time

metrics.name: Voluntary attrition
metrics.why: First departures set a precedent and signal cultural cracks

metrics.name: Pulse survey response rate
metrics.why: Low response means people don't trust surveys lead to change

metrics.name: Manager 1:1 consistency
metrics.why: Skipped 1:1s are the most common disengagement accelerator

startup.dx: Track developer satisfaction alongside velocity. Teams with poor DevEx have higher attrition.

scaleup.reality: Attrition becomes a number to manage. DEI matters. Manager quality drives retention. Burnout risk increases with scale.

goals.text: Manager effectiveness program: all EMs trained
DEI goals with measurable targets
Burnout detection framework (signals, interventions)
Total rewards philosophy: comp + equity + benefits + growth

metrics.name: Voluntary attrition
metrics.why: At scale, attrition rate directly impacts delivery capacity

metrics.name: Manager effectiveness
metrics.why: People leave managers, not companies, especially at scale

metrics.name: DEI representation
metrics.why: Representation gaps widen without active measurement

metrics.name: Engagement score
metrics.why: Tracks whether growth is energizing or exhausting the team

metrics.name: Regrettable attrition
metrics.why: Losing top performers costs 2x their salary to replace

scaleup.dx: SPACE framework: Satisfaction alongside Productivity. Measure both. Optimize for sustainable performance.

growth.reality: Engagement at scale. People analytics team. Predictive attrition models. Culture as a product.

goals.text: Predictive attrition model identifies flight risk 90 days out
Engineering culture score tracked and improved quarterly
Flexible work policy that actually works
Wellbeing program: mental health, ergonomics, sabbaticals

metrics.name: Attrition prediction accuracy
metrics.why: Accurate prediction enables proactive, not reactive, retention

metrics.name: Culture score
metrics.why: Culture left unmeasured drifts from intention to accident

metrics.name: Regrettable attrition
metrics.why: Distinguishes healthy turnover from talent hemorrhaging

metrics.name: Wellbeing NPS
metrics.why: Wellbeing programs only matter if engineers actually value them

metrics.name: Flexible work satisfaction
metrics.why: Flexibility policy gaps drive attrition to remote-friendly competitors

growth.dx: Developer experience surveys become part of the culture measurement framework. Happy devs = retained devs.

enterprise.reality: Global workforce. Multi-generational. Union considerations in some geos. Culture across acquisitions.

goals.text: Global engagement strategy with local adaptation
Post-acquisition culture integration playbook
Multi-generational workforce strategy
Alumni network as strategic asset

metrics.name: Global engagement
metrics.why: Engagement variance across regions reveals systemic inequity

metrics.name: Acquisition retention (1yr)
metrics.why: Failed retention post-acquisition destroys deal value

metrics.name: Regional engagement parity
metrics.why: Parity gaps expose HQ-centric culture blind spots

metrics.name: Alumni engagement rate
metrics.why: Active alumni become referrers, customers, and boomerang hires

metrics.name: Regrettable attrition
metrics.why: At enterprise scale, even small percentage points are hundreds of people

enterprise.dx: At forest scale, culture is fractal. Each team has its own microculture within the global framework.

label: Evolve
core: How engineers transition out gracefully and become alumni advocates

seed.reality: Every departure feels personal. There's no process. You buy them a drink and wish them well.

goals.text: Honest exit conversation with founder
Clean offboarding: access revoked, knowledge transferred
Part on good terms — every leaver is a future referrer

metrics.name: Knowledge transfer completion
metrics.why: Incomplete transfers create knowledge voids in a tiny team

metrics.name: Access revocation time
metrics.why: Lingering access is a security risk you can't afford

metrics.name: Exit sentiment
metrics.why: Every leaver becomes a recruiter or a warning for others

seed.dx: Document as you go. If one person's departure causes chaos, your bus factor is too low.

startup.reality: First structured offboarding. Exit interviews start. Alumni Slack channel appears. Knowledge gaps after departures expose documentation debt.

goals.text: Structured exit interview process with themes tracked
30-day knowledge transfer plan for every departure
Alumni network: Slack/LinkedIn group for former engineers
Automated access revocation on last day

metrics.name: Exit interview completion
metrics.why: Missing exit data means you repeat the same retention mistakes

metrics.name: Knowledge transfer score
metrics.why: Quality of handoff determines team disruption after departure

metrics.name: Access revocation (auto)
metrics.why: Manual revocation at scale guarantees security gaps

metrics.name: Alumni channel join rate
metrics.why: High join rate signals people leave the role, not the community

startup.dx: Every departure that causes a knowledge gap is a documentation failure. Use departures to improve docs.

scaleup.reality: Attrition data becomes actionable. Exit themes drive org changes. Boomerang hires start happening. Offboarding is systematic.

goals.text: Exit theme analysis drives quarterly action items
Boomerang hire program: former engineers return
Automated offboarding across all systems (< 1 day)
Internal mobility as alternative to departure

metrics.name: Exit themes → actions
metrics.why: Themes without actions make exit interviews performative

metrics.name: Boomerang hire rate
metrics.why: Returning engineers validate your culture and skip ramp time

metrics.name: Offboarding automation
metrics.why: Manual offboarding at scale creates compliance and security holes

metrics.name: Internal transfer saves
metrics.why: Every internal save avoids a costly external replacement cycle

metrics.name: Avg knowledge transfer score
metrics.why: Consistent handoff quality prevents post-departure fire drills

scaleup.dx: Offboarding should be as smooth as onboarding. Both are DevEx moments that define your reputation.

growth.reality: Offboarding is a product. Alumni network drives referrals. Exit data informs org strategy. Transitions are dignified.

goals.text: Alumni referral program generating 10%+ of pipeline
Exit data integrated into retention strategy
Graceful layoff/RIF process (if needed) with support
Alumni events: annual reunion, ongoing community

metrics.name: Alumni referral pipeline
metrics.why: Alumni who refer prove the experience was worth recommending

metrics.name: Exit → retention actions
metrics.why: Closing the exit-to-action loop makes departures drive improvement

metrics.name: Alumni event attendance
metrics.why: Attendance measures whether your alumni network has real gravity

metrics.name: Offboarding NPS
metrics.why: Bad offboarding taints the entire tenure in retrospect

metrics.name: Glassdoor exit rating
metrics.why: Exit reviews are the most-read and most-trusted by candidates

growth.dx: Your public IDP and eng blog keep alumni connected to your engineering culture even after they leave.

enterprise.reality: Global offboarding compliance. Strategic workforce planning. Alumni as ecosystem. Transitions across BUs before external exit.

goals.text: Global offboarding compliance across all jurisdictions
Alumni ecosystem: advisory, investing, referrals, re-hires
Strategic workforce planning: predict and shape attrition
Cross-BU transfer program reduces external attrition by 15%+

metrics.name: Global compliance
metrics.why: Non-compliance in any jurisdiction creates enterprise-wide liability

metrics.name: Alumni ecosystem value
metrics.why: Unmeasured alumni programs can't justify continued investment

metrics.name: Workforce plan accuracy
metrics.why: Planning accuracy enables proactive rather than reactive hiring

metrics.name: Cross-BU transfer rate
metrics.why: Internal transfers preserve institutional knowledge others lose

metrics.name: Boomerang rate
metrics.why: High boomerang rate is the ultimate employer brand proof point

enterprise.dx: Enterprise alumni often become customers, partners, or investors. The developer lifecycle extends beyond employment.
